title: The History of Coffee
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Coffee is one of the most popular beverages in the world, but few people are (0) of its fascinating origins. According to legend, coffee was first discovered in Ethiopia when a goat herder (1) that his animals became unusually energetic after eating certain berries. The drink gradually (2) across the Arabian Peninsula, where it (3) popularity in the fifteenth century. Coffee houses, known as 'qahveh khaneh', began to (4) up in cities throughout the Middle East. These establishments quickly became important centres for social (5) and the exchange of information. By the seventeenth century, coffee had (6) its way to Europe, where it was initially met with suspicion. However, it soon won people over and became a (7) of daily life. Today, over two billion cups of coffee are consumed every day, making it one of the most (8) commodities on the planet.

example.explanation: 'Be aware of' es la expresión correcta para indicar conocimiento de algo.

questions.explanation: 'Noticed' indica percibir algo de forma casual, sin intención previa.
'Spread' es el verbo adecuado para la difusión de costumbres o productos.
La colocación correcta es 'gained popularity', que significa 'ganar popularidad'.
'Spring up' es un phrasal verb que significa aparecer o surgir rápidamente.
'Social gatherings' es la colocación natural para reuniones sociales informales.
La expresión fija es 'made its way to', que significa 'llegar a' o 'abrirse camino hacia'.
'Staple' se usa para algo esencial o fundamental en la vida diaria.
'Sought-after' es la expresión correcta para algo muy deseado o demandado.
